title: Keys to Success with the SAAR | Analysis Resources | NHSN | CDC

Top of Page SAAR Baselines NHSN currently offers three SAAR baselines, each applicable to a select set of patient care locations and time periods. The table below provides a high-level overview of each baseline.
Table providing a high-level overview of each SAAR baseline 
SAAR Baseline 
Locations 
Antimicrobial Categories 
Dates 
2014 baseline adult and pediatric SAARs* 
6 adult and pediatric intensive care units (ICUs) and wards 
5 antimicrobial categories 
January 1, 2014 – December 31, 2018 
2017 baseline adult and pediatric SAARs 
13 adult and pediatric medical, surgical, medical-surgical ICUs, wards, step down units, and oncology units 
15 antimicrobial categories 
January 1, 2017 – present 
2018 baseline neonatal SAARs 
4 neonatal units, including step-down nurseries and neonatal critical care units 
7 antimicrobial categories 
January 1, 2018 – present 
*You cannot directly compare SAARs calculated under the 2014 baseline with 2017 baseline SAAR values.
You can find reports based on each of the SAAR baselines in Analysis Reports, within the Antimicrobial Use and Resistance (AUR) Module folder.
Top of Page Troubleshooting Are you getting the “No Records Met Your Criteria” message when you try to generate a SAAR? Try these quick fixes below to get your SAAR reports up and runn
